title: The Witch's Brew
overview:
“The Witch’s Brew” presents a decision-based narrative action-adventure RPG that combines branching dialogue, quest-driven progression, and exploration-based gameplay.

The core mechanic of the game is player choice, where dialogue decisions directly influence quest availability, character relationships, and ultimately the ending the player receives.

The game is structured around a dual-path system—combat-focused and cozy/community-focused progression—allowing players to uncover different aspects of the island’s central mystery across multiple playthroughs.

These narrative systems are supported by action-adventure mechanics such as real-time combat, crafting, resource gathering, and potion brewing, ensuring a balance between story progression and interactive gameplay.
contribution:
As a fully independent project, all ideas, systems, dialogue, level design, communication design and macro systems for “The Witch’s Brew” were created and implemented by me.

We were permitted to use pre-existing assets from sources such as the Unity Asset Store and Itch.io.

I commissioned friend and composer Daniel Da Silva to create the unique soundtrack for my game.
process:
The project was developed iteratively over the course of six months, with significant refinement of systems such as inventory management, quest tracking, dialogue branching, combat feedback, and progression pacing to improve clarity, usability, and narrative cohesion.

Key improvements included a redesigned inventory system with stacking and item dropping, a quest sidebar with directional indicators, converging branching dialogue structures, and enhanced combat feedback through health bars and audiovisual effects.

A structured day-night progression system and expanded quest types further strengthened pacing and narrative flow, while reward systems such as spells and potion recipes tied mechanical progression directly to story advancement.
